metadata.intent: Minimal predator-prey HOPF scenario (issue #358; Research Brief F, AC5 cross-check 2). One producer cluster (a sparse, slow-reproducing standing crop) + one consumer cluster within trophic-transfer reach, nothing else -- the smallest world that carries the frozen<->oscillation coordinate (Brief F, AC1: this mode lives on a producer<->consumer coupling, a 2x2 Jacobian, not a trait grid). The throwaway compartment-ODE + Jacobian prototype (crates/explorers-sim/src/bin/hopf_prototype.rs) reads THIS file's parameters and two clusters, assembles the mean-field reduction of the committed tick map T from committed fluxes only, and predicts the base_trophic_efficiency at which the leading eigenvalue pair crosses the unit circle (a Neimark-Sacker / discrete Hopf bifurcation). Committed AT base_trophic_efficiency = 0.8 (deliberately ABOVE the predicted crossing), so F predicts a LIMIT CYCLE. The headless eval_scenarios sweep across the crossing is the falsifiable validation; the verdict is docs/research/F-hopf-validation.md.
metadata.roster: 18 producers (photosynthetic_absorption 0.5, sessile, deliberately LOW reproduction -- fecundity 0.1, asexual 0.05, dispersal 0.5 -- so the prey form a near-flat standing crop rather than a self-pulsing cohort) spread over a ~28-unit field; 3 consumers (heterotrophy 0.55, lightly mobile at 0.15 so they can track prey, kappa 0.45) near the field centre, within feeding reach. No decomposers, no detrital deposit -- a pure two-trophic-level loop.
metadata.rationale: F's mean-field reading -- the 2-compartment producer<->consumer map assembled from photosynthesise+metabolise+grow as the selection diagonal, resolve_drains (phase.rs:386) as the bilinear trophic term (the Hopf seat), and death (phase.rs:687) as the removal diagonal -- predicts a Neimark-Sacker crossing in base_trophic_efficiency at base* = 0.4427 (closed form base* = (1+m)/(kappa_C*gamma*a*K_P*exp(-decay*d)); derived coefficients: d=0.959, r_P=1.30, K_P=71.1, a=0.423, m=0.113, kappa_C=0.45, gamma=0.3; see hopf_prototype output and docs/research/F-hopf-validation.md). Below base* the interior coexistence point is a stable spiral (FREEZE -> low oscillation_strength); above it a limit cycle (OSCILLATE -> high oscillation_strength). This file sits at base_trophic_efficiency = 0.8 (above base*), so F predicts OSCILLATION. The prediction is an existence/stability claim only (Brief F, AC3) -- it does NOT claim any per-seed distributional outcome. OBSERVED (docs/research/F-hopf-validation.md): the headless oscillation_strength sweep shows NO transition at base*; it sits at a ~0.3-0.4 noise floor across the whole physical range, set by finite-N producer reproduction pulsing rather than the trophic coupling. This disagreement is a DIAGNOSTIC SIGNAL, not a defect in either lens: it localises to (1) the finite-N demographic stochasticity the mean field erases (Brief F's own AC3 boundary) and (2) the spatial in-reach geometry, which the well-mixed mass-action B(n,n) reading does not realise.
metadata.geometry: Producers occupy a ~28-unit field (radius out to ~17); consumers sit near the centroid and are lightly mobile (mobility 0.15) so they can roam toward prey -- the spatial realisation of the well-mixed mass-action coupling the prototype assumes. Consumption reach = eff_heterotrophy * contact_range_coefficient = 0.55 * 5.0 = 2.75 units (body_reach_coefficient = 0, structure-independent). Mobility is kept LOW so the consumer's trait-space distance to the producer (which the committed trophic kernel exp(-decay*d) penalises) does not inflate and decouple the pair -- the central modelling tension the verdict records.
metadata.status: current -- every WorldParameter specified explicitly (example9 template). base_metabolic_rate is low (0.1) and trophic_distance_decay is low (0.5) so the Neimark-Sacker crossing falls mid-range inside the physical efficiency interval (0,1]; with the defaults (0.3 / 1.0) the predicted crossing exceeds 1 (F predicts unconditional stability) and no transition is even nominally observable.
